description_long: This is a delicate flowering plant with a slender, pale green stem supporting several broad, ovate leaves arranged alternately along its length. At the top, a cluster of small, creamy-white florets forms a dense inflorescence, surrounded by four large, showy, white petals that are widely spaced and slightly overlapping, resembling those of a Hydrangea. The petals are flat and thin, with a soft, matte finish, and the central cluster of tiny flowers adds a textured, intricate appearance. The leaves are deep green, smooth-edged, and have a slightly glossy surface, providing a striking contrast to the pale flowers. The overall structure is airy and lightweight, with visible gaps between the leaves and petals, and the stem appears flexible but sturdy. This flower would commonly be found in urban parks, gardens, or landscaped sidewalks, adding a touch of natural beauty to the environment.
description: A slender-stemmed flowering plant with broad green leaves and a cluster of small white florets surrounded by four large, flat white petals. The arrangement is airy and delicate, with a striking contrast between the deep green leaves and pale flowers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full cluster of white florets and all four large, white petals, with leaves clearly visible below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered arrangement of petals and the vertical alignment of the stem and leaves.
description_view_2: Rear angled view showing the backs of the petals and the dense green foliage, with the flower cluster partially obscured.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the overlapping petals and the symmetrical spread of the leaves.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled configuration, with four large, flat petals radiating symmetrically around the central cluster of small florets. The petals are slightly overlapping at their bases and spaced evenly, creating a balanced, radial symmetry typical of many ornamental flowers.
